method: Multi-node DHT discovery tested with in-memory CapabilityDht. 3 agents with real ML-DSA-65 keypairs on simulated different networks (10.0.1.1, 10.0.2.1, 10.0.3.1). Churn test with 10 agents joining, 5 leaving, 3 rejoining. Real multi-machine DHT pending Track R (WAN Discovery).

results.agent_offline_removal: PASS — agent 2 removed, inference count drops from 2 to 1
results.agent_rejoin: PASS — agent 2 re-announced, inference count back to 2

multi_node_discovery.status: PASS — all announce, lookup, offline, rejoin operations succeed

churn_handling.notes: Join time includes ML-DSA-65 keypair generation (24.6ms per agent). Leave and lookup are sub-millisecond.

observations: DHT announce: 15.7ms for 3 agents (includes 3x ML-DSA-65 keypair generation + signing)
DHT lookup: 0.005ms (sub-microsecond) — in-memory hash table lookup
Agent removal: instant — hash table deletion
Churn: 246ms for 10 agents joining (dominated by keypair generation, not DHT operations)
Real multi-machine DHT routing (Kademlia-style) is Track R — R1 partially implemented (uncommitted dht_router.rs)
verify: Multi-node DHT discovery works over simulated WAN. 3 agents with 4 capabilities, announce/lookup/offline/rejoin all pass. Churn handling with 10 agents validated.
